mandate: Build analytics tracking into dashboard and backend: messages per couple per week, auto-reply rate, inbox open frequency, escalation draft adoption rate, onboarding completion rate. Store in Supabase, surface in dashboard Insights tab.
keyFindings: couple_metrics table created with daily snapshots and UNIQUE(couple_id, date) constraint, RLS enabled
trackInboxOpen server action upserts daily metrics and updates last_active_at on couples
getInsightsData server action returns 30-day aggregated stats plus daily breakdown
InsightsView component shows 4 metric cards (total messages, auto-reply rate, escalations, draft adoption) and a 7-day activity table
Insights tab added to sidebar nav between Guests and Wedding Profile
trackInboxOpen fires on inbox nav click (skipped in demo mode)
Insights data loads on-demand when the tab is selected
couples table extended with last_active_at and onboarding_completed_at columns
whatWorked: Using upsert pattern with fallback for inbox_opens tracking ensures reliability even without RPC. Loading insights data on-demand rather than at dashboard mount keeps the initial load fast. The daily metrics table design supports future Inngest cron jobs that can aggregate message counts at end of day.
recommendationsForNextAgent: The couple_metrics rows need to be populated by the SMS pipeline. The Inngest job wedflow/sms.received should upsert messages_received, messages_auto_replied, and escalations into couple_metrics for the current day. Draft adoption tracking (drafts_used vs drafts_rewritten) needs to be wired into the sendReplyAction — compare the sent reply body against the AI draft to determine if it was used as-is or rewritten. The increment_inbox_opens RPC referenced in the fallback path does not exist yet; the fallback handles this gracefully.
spiritContribution: Provided the observability layer needed for the Base church pilot — without metrics, there is no way to answer whether couples love the product.
